title: [Bug]: Backlog relationship sync sends dependency issue id as text
summary: The managed backlog sync workflow can fail while adding GitHub issue dependencies because the GitHub CLI call sends issue_id as a string value instead of an integer value.
observed: When syncing a backlog item with a dependency relationship, GitHub rejects the dependency request because issue_id is not submitted as an integer.
expected: Backlog relationship sync should submit issue_id as an integer so GitHub can create native issue dependency relationships when the repository supports them.
reproduction: Run the focused issue-planning relationship test and verify that the generated gh api command uses the integer field form for issue_id.
impact: Feature and bug issue bodies are still created, but native GitHub issue relationships may fail to sync, weakening backlog navigation and release planning evidence.
delivery_semantics: No runtime delivery behavior changes. This fix affects GitHub issue automation only.
security: The fix must not expose private repository details, credentials, URLs, IP addresses, tokens, or issue comments containing sensitive deployment information.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves issue dependency creation uses an integer issue_id form.
Backlog sync can continue after relationship fields are present.
Existing issue planning tests continue to pass.
The changelog records the automation fix.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md because this changes release-planning automation behavior.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include regression evidence, full validation evidence, and any remaining limitations.
